name: Chorizo Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. If necessary, remove the sausage casing and coarsely chop the chorizo until you have about 1 cup. Set aside.
2. In a bowl, combine flour, Parmesan cheese, sugar, baking powder, fennel seeds, salt, and baking soda.
3. In another bowl, beat cream cheese until smooth; then stir in milk. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Stir in butter and the chopped chorizo.
4. Gradually add the flour mixture to the cream cheese batter, stirring just until moistened.
5. Pour the batter into a greased 5 x 9 inch loaf pan.
6. Bake in a 375°F oven until the bread is browned and begins to pull away from the sides of the pan (about 55 minutes).
7. Let cool in the pan on a rack for 5 minutes before turning out onto a wire rack. Cut into thick slices and serve warm. Store at room temperature for up to 1 day, or freeze for longer storage. Thaw wrapped and reheat uncovered in a 350°F oven.
